subject: Mathematics
q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>10</mn><mo>°</mo></mrow></mfenced><mi>sin</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>20</mn><mo>°</mo></mrow></mfenced><mi>sin</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>40</mn><mo>°</mo></mrow></mfenced><mi>sin</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>50</mn><mo>°</mo></mrow></mfenced><mi>sin</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>70</mn><mo>°</mo></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>-</mo></math> <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>16</mn></mfrac><mi>sin</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>10</mn><mo>°</mo></mrow></mfenced></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>16</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>α</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> is equal to _____.
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>10</mn><mo>°</mo></mrow></mfenced><mi>sin</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>20</mn><mo>°</mo></mrow></mfenced><mi>sin</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>40</mn><mo>°</mo></mrow></mfenced><mi>sin</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>50</mn><mo>°</mo></mrow></mfenced><mi>sin</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>70</mn><mo>°</mo></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>-</mo></math> <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>16</mn></mfrac><mi>sin</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>10</mn><mo>°</mo></mrow></mfenced></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>16</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>α</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> is equal to _____.
solution:
We have, sin210°×sin20°×sin40°×sin50°×sin70°

=sin10°×sin50°×sin70°sin10°×sin20°×sin40°

Using, sinθ=cos90°-θ

= cos80°×cos40°×cos20°sin10°×sin30°-10°×sin30°+10°

We know that cosθcos2θcos22θ…cos2n-1θ=sin2nθ2nsinθ

and sinA+B·sinA-B=sin2A-sin2B⇒  sin2320°23sin20°sin10°×14-sin210°

⇒  sin160°23sin20°sin10°-4sin310°4

⇒  sin180°-20°8sin20°3sin10°-4sin310°-2sin10°4

⇒  sin20°8sin20°sin30°-2sin10°4

⇒  1812-2sin10°4

⇒  181-4sin10°8=α-sin10°16   (given)

⇒  1-4sin10°64=16α-sin10°16

⇒  1-4sin10°=64α-4sin10°

⇒  α=164.

subject: Mathematics
q:
Let $\frac{\pi}{2}<\theta<\pi$ and $\cot \theta=-\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{2}}$. Then the value of
$\sin \left(\frac{15 \theta}{2}\right)(\cos 8 \theta+\sin 8 \theta)+\cos \left(\frac{15 \theta}{2}\right)(\cos 8 \theta-\sin 8 \theta)$
is equal to
question:
Let $\frac{\pi}{2}<\theta<\pi$ and $\cot \theta=-\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{2}}$. Then the value of
$\sin \left(\frac{15 \theta}{2}\right)(\cos 8 \theta+\sin 8 \theta)+\cos \left(\frac{15 \theta}{2}\right)(\cos 8 \theta-\sin 8 \theta)$
is equal to
solution:
Expanding and regrouping:
$\sin\frac{15\theta}{2}(\cos 8\theta + \sin 8\theta) + \cos\frac{15\theta}{2}(\cos 8\theta - \sin 8\theta)$
$= \left(\cos\frac{15\theta}{2}\cos 8\theta + \sin\frac{15\theta}{2}\sin 8\theta\right) + \left(\sin\frac{15\theta}{2}\cos 8\theta - \cos\frac{15\theta}{2}\sin 8\theta\right)$
$= \cos\left(8\theta - \frac{15\theta}{2}\right) + \sin\left(\frac{15\theta}{2} - 8\theta\right) = \cos\frac{\theta}{2} - \sin\frac{\theta}{2}$.
From $\cot\theta = -\frac{1}{2\sqrt{2}}$: $\sin\theta = \frac{2\sqrt{2}}{3}$, $\cos\theta = -\frac{1}{3}$.
$\left(\cos\frac{\theta}{2} - \sin\frac{\theta}{2}\right)^2 = 1 - \sin\theta = 1 - \frac{2\sqrt{2}}{3} = \frac{(\sqrt{2}-1)^2}{3}$.
Since $\frac{\pi}{4} < \frac{\theta}{2} < \frac{\pi}{2}$, $\sin\frac{\theta}{2} > \cos\frac{\theta}{2}$, so the value is $\frac{1-\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}}$.

subject: Mathematics
q: Let $P = \{\theta \in [0, 4\pi] : \tan^2\theta \neq 1\}$ and $S = \{a \in \mathbb{Z} : 2(\cos^8\theta - \sin^8\theta)\sec 2\theta = a^2, \theta \in P\}$. Then $n(S)$ is:
question: Let $P = \{\theta \in [0, 4\pi] : \tan^2\theta \neq 1\}$ and $S = \{a \in \mathbb{Z} : 2(\cos^8\theta - \sin^8\theta)\sec 2\theta = a^2, \theta \in P\}$. Then $n(S)$ is:
solution:
Given the expression $2(\cos^8 heta - \sin^8 heta)\sec 2 heta = a^2$.

We can simplify the term $\cos^8 heta - \sin^8 heta$ as follows:
$\cos^8 heta - \sin^8 heta = (\cos^4 heta - \sin^4 heta)(\cos^4 heta + \sin^4 heta)$
$\Rightarrow (\cos^2 heta - \sin^2 heta)(\cos^2 heta + \sin^2 heta)((\cos^2 heta + \sin^2 heta)^2 - 2\sin^2 heta\cos^2 heta)$
$\Rightarrow \cos 2 heta imes 1 imes \left(1 - \dfrac{1}{2}\sin^2 2 heta ight)$

Substitute this back into the given equation:
$2 \left[ \cos 2 heta \left(1 - \dfrac{1}{2}\sin^2 2 heta ight) ight] \dfrac{1}{\cos 2 heta} = a^2$

Since $ heta \in P$, we have $ an^2 heta eq 1$, which implies $\cos 2 heta eq 0$. Thus, we can safely cancel $\cos 2 heta$:
$2 \left(1 - \dfrac{1}{2}\sin^2 2 heta ight) = a^2$
$\Rightarrow 2 - \sin^2 2 heta = a^2$

We know that for any real $ heta$, $0 \le \sin^2 2 heta \le 1$.
However, since $ an^2 heta eq 1$, $\sin^2 2 heta eq 1$.
Therefore, $0 \le \sin^2 2 heta < 1$.

Multiplying by $-1$ and adding $2$, we get the range of $a^2$:
$1 < 2 - \sin^2 2 heta \le 2$
$\Rightarrow 1 < a^2 \le 2$

Since $a \in \mathbb{Z}$, $a^2$ must be a perfect square integer. The only integer in the interval $(1, 2]$ is $2$, so $a^2 = 2$.

But there is no integer $a$ such that $a^2 = 2$.

Thus, the set $S$ contains no elements, meaning $S = \emptyset$.

Therefore, $n(S) = 0$.

Answer: $0$

question: If <math><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>5</mn></mrow></mfrac><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>,</mo><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">sin</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>)</mo></mrow></mrow><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>5</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>13</mn></mrow></mfrac></math> and <math><mn>0</mn><mo><</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi><mo><</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>π</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>,</mo></math> then <math><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">tan</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>α</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mrow></math> is equal to:
solution:
cosα+β=35⇒tanα+β=43

sin(α-β)=513⇒tan(α-β)=512

Now,

tan2α=tanα+β+α-β

            =tan(α+β)+tan(α-β)1-tan(α+β)tan(α-β)

            =43+5121-43×512=6316

q: If $\frac{\pi}{2} \leq x \leq \frac{3 \pi}{4}$, then $\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{12}{13} \cos x+\frac{5}{13} \sin x\right)$ is equal to
question: If $\frac{\pi}{2} \leq x \leq \frac{3 \pi}{4}$, then $\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{12}{13} \cos x+\frac{5}{13} \sin x\right)$ is equal to
solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{12}{13} \cos x+\frac{5}{13} \sin x \\ & \text { Let } \tan \alpha=\frac{5}{12}, \alpha \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right) \\ & \Rightarrow \sin \alpha=\frac{5}{13}, \cos \alpha=\frac{12}{13}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \frac{12}{13} \cos x+\frac{5}{13} \sin x=\cos \alpha \cos x+\sin \alpha \sin x \\ & \quad=\cos (x-\alpha) \\ & \Rightarrow \cos ^{-1}[\cos (x-\alpha)]=x-\alpha \\ & \quad=x-\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{5}{12}\right)\end{aligned}$

q: If $A = \dfrac{\sin 3^\circ}{\cos 9^\circ} + \dfrac{\sin 9^\circ}{\cos 27^\circ} + \dfrac{\sin 27^\circ}{\cos 81^\circ}$ and $B = \tan 81^\circ - \tan 3^\circ$, then $\dfrac{B}{A}$ is equal to _____.
question: If $A = \dfrac{\sin 3^\circ}{\cos 9^\circ} + \dfrac{\sin 9^\circ}{\cos 27^\circ} + \dfrac{\sin 27^\circ}{\cos 81^\circ}$ and $B = \tan 81^\circ - \tan 3^\circ$, then $\dfrac{B}{A}$ is equal to _____.
solution:
Consider the general term $\dfrac{\sin x}{\cos 3x}$.

We can rewrite this by multiplying and dividing by $2\cos x$:

$\dfrac{2\sin x \cos x}{2\cos 3x \cos x} = \dfrac{\sin 2x}{2\cos 3x \cos x}$

Using $\sin 2x = \sin(3x - x) = \sin 3x \cos x - \cos 3x \sin x$, we get:

$\dfrac{\sin 3x \cos x - \cos 3x \sin x}{2\cos 3x \cos x} = \dfrac{1}{2} \left( \dfrac{\sin 3x}{\cos 3x} - \dfrac{\sin x}{\cos x} \right) = \dfrac{1}{2} (\tan 3x - \tan x)$

Applying this identity to each term of $A$:

$\dfrac{\sin 3^\circ}{\cos 9^\circ} = \dfrac{1}{2} (\tan 9^\circ - \tan 3^\circ)$

$\dfrac{\sin 9^\circ}{\cos 27^\circ} = \dfrac{1}{2} (\tan 27^\circ - \tan 9^\circ)$

$\dfrac{\sin 27^\circ}{\cos 81^\circ} = \dfrac{1}{2} (\tan 81^\circ - \tan 27^\circ)$

Adding these terms, the series telescopes:

$A = \dfrac{1}{2} (\tan 9^\circ - \tan 3^\circ + \tan 27^\circ - \tan 9^\circ + \tan 81^\circ - \tan 27^\circ)$

$A = \dfrac{1}{2} (\tan 81^\circ - \tan 3^\circ)$

We are given $B = \tan 81^\circ - \tan 3^\circ$.

Substituting $B$ into the equation for $A$:

$A = \dfrac{1}{2} B$

$\Rightarrow \dfrac{B}{A} = 2$

Answer: $2$

q: Let $f_{k}(x)=\frac{1}{k}\left(\sin ^{k} x+\cos ^{k} x\right)$ for $\mathrm{k}=1,2,3, \ldots$ Then for all $\mathrm{x} \in \mathrm{R},$ the value of $f_{4}(x)-f_{6}(x)$ is equal to :
question: Let $f_{k}(x)=\frac{1}{k}\left(\sin ^{k} x+\cos ^{k} x\right)$ for $\mathrm{k}=1,2,3, \ldots$ Then for all $\mathrm{x} \in \mathrm{R},$ the value of $f_{4}(x)-f_{6}(x)$ is equal to :
solution:
$f_{k}(x)=\frac{1}{k}\left(\sin ^{k} x+\cos ^{k} x\right)$

$f_{4}(x)=\frac{1}{4}\left[\sin ^{4} x+\cos ^{4} x\right]$

$=\frac{1}{4}\left[\left(\sin ^{2} x+\cos ^{2} x\right)^{2}-\frac{(\sin 2 x)^{2}}{2}\right]$

$=\frac{1}{4}\left[1-\frac{(\sin 2 x)^{2}}{2}\right]$

$f_{6}(x)=\frac{1}{6}\left[\sin ^{6} x+\cos ^{6} x\right]$

$=\frac{1}{6}\left[\left(\sin ^{2} x+\left(\cos ^{2} x\right)-\frac{3}{4}\left(\sin ^{2} x\right)^{2}\right]\right.$

$=\frac{1}{6}\left[1-\frac{3}{4}(\sin 2 x)^{2}\right]$

Now $f_{4}(x)-f_{(6)}(x)=\frac{1}{4}-\frac{1}{6}-\frac{(\sin 2 x)^{2}}{8}+\frac{1}{8}(\sin 2 x)^{2}$

$=\frac{1}{12}$

question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>cot</mi><mi>α</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>sec</mi><mi>β</mi><mo>=</mo><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mn>5</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>π</mi><mo><</mo><mi>α</mi><mo><</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo><</mo><mi>β</mi><mo><</mo><mi>π</mi></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>tan</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> and the quadrant in which <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi></math> lies, respectively are
solution:
Given,

cotα=1,secβ=-53

So, cosβ=-35,tanβ=-43 and tanα=1

Now using formula tanα+β=tanα+tanβ1-tanαtanβ

⇒tanα+β=1-431+43×1=-17 

We know that tanθ is negative in 2nd & 4th  quadrant but as π<α<3π2, so α+β will lie in IVth quadrant.

q: If <math><mi>m</mi></math> and <math><mi>M</mi></math> are the minimum and the maximum values of <math><mn>4</mn><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac><mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">sin</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">cos</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mrow><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>x</mi><mi> </mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then <math><mi>M</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>m</mi></math> is equal to:
question: If <math><mi>m</mi></math> and <math><mi>M</mi></math> are the minimum and the maximum values of <math><mn>4</mn><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac><mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">sin</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">cos</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mrow><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>x</mi><mi> </mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then <math><mi>M</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>m</mi></math> is equal to:
solution:
Given expression is 4+12sin2⁡2x-2cos4⁡x

=  4+21-cos2⁡xcos2⁡x-2cos4⁡x

Creating perfect squares, we get

=-4 cos2⁡x-142-1716 

Since, 0 ≤cos2⁡x ≤1

⇒ -14 ≤cos2⁡x-14 ≤34

⇒ 0 ≤cos2⁡x-142 ≤916

⇒ -1716 ≤cos2⁡x-142-1716 ≤916-1716

⇒ 174 ≥-4 cos2⁡x-142-1716 ≥2

⇒ M=174 and m=2
⇒ M-m=174-2=94

q: The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>cos</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>7</mn></mfrac></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mn>4</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>7</mn></mfrac></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mn>6</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>7</mn></mfrac></mfenced></math> is equal to
question: The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>cos</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>7</mn></mfrac></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mn>4</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>7</mn></mfrac></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mn>6</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>7</mn></mfrac></mfenced></math> is equal to
solution:
Given, cos2π7+cos4π7+cos6π7

=12sinπ72cos2π7sinπ7+2cos4π7sinπ7+2cos6π7sinπ7

=12sinπ7sin3π7-sinπ7+sin5π7-sin3π7+sin7π7-sin5π7

Since, 2cosAsinB=sinA+B-sinA-B

=12sinπ7-sinπ7+sinπ

=-12, ∵sinπ=0
